Because if we have a belief system that selling is something you do to people, how good are we going to be at sailing? Not very good. Because you're going to feel nervous. Now, when you have knowledge, what do you have? You have power. You have full control. You're going to get fired?
No, because you're outselling everybody else. You will be the last one in that company. I mean, the owner's firing their spouse before they fire you. You are the last one. You are the most valuable because you control everything because of your what? knowledge, and you contain power. On the flip side, when you don't have that, you have no control. You let outside factors control you. And so when the economy goes down and sales start to dry up, who determines your fate? your boss. because you have no control. You have no power. I love purple hair. My daughter has purple hair. It's awesome. Okay?
What if it did work for you? What if it did? Are you against that? Who's against that? Anybody? No, right? Okay. Your prospects have frames. They have objections. They have objections about money. They have objections that it's going to cost too much. They say it's too expensive. If you can't overcome that yourself, how in the hell are you going to help them overcome themselves? If you can't overcome your frame, your way of thinking that holds you here, how in the hell are you going to learn how to help your prospects overcome that way of thinking? You can't. It's not congruent. Would you agree? Raise your hand if you agree with that, okay? Because that's why I'm here. That's why I started this company. Selling is not something you do.
Two people, it's something you do. Four people. So how are you going to help your prospects overcome their objections, their way of thinking, if you can't overcome? Your own. Do you see why you're stuck where you're at? Because our thinking keeps us in the same frame. And until we overcome that way of thinking, until we get to the other side, because... We all have a fear of change, right? But what's on the other side of change? What's on the other side of change? Do you ever have a fear of changing something? Maybe you're in a bad relationship. You're like, I can't have to stay with him. I'm afraid to leave. And then somehow you mustered up the courage and you left. And what did you decide, what did you find on the other side?
Change. Greatness. Everything you've ever wanted. You just didn't see it on the other side until you got there. So who in here? When I'm up here for an hour and a half later today, wants to learn how to overcome that way of thinking so you can help your prospects overcome that way of thinking. Raise your hand if that's you, please. Okay, right-hand corner, left-hand corner. Do I run by left-hand corner, people? I'm coming for you. I see you over here. You're guilty. I saw you. Okay, Ryan, does that help a little bit?
